pages.full_text:
[postcard of Tournon-sur-Rhône glued in]

The university was founded by Cardinal Tourmon.

Monday

Having left the hotel, we motor-wayed to Lyons & spent an hour trying to find a parking place without Success. There's a rail strike & they've blocked the main square from parking. It would have been nice to have a meal.

We have come to a place the Same distance north of Lyons (as Tournon) we're now at a rainy
photo_captions: The university was founded by Cardinal Tourmon.
pages.notes: Page continues onto right page. The postcard shows 'Tournon-sur-Rhône'. The handwritten caption reads 'Cardinal Tourmon' (likely meaning Cardinal de Tournon). No handwritten page number visible at top-left corner. Year cannot be determined from this page alone; volume covers 2003–2019.

pages.full_text:
__Tournous__, an important Mediaeval Monastery town.

We had a delicious supper after looking around the abbey. Simon had cheese & salad & I had murdered eggs (vinegar.) The plates were really hot. Then Simon had steak & I had chicken. Then Simon had crème brûlée & I had chocolate mousse. A really good meal in our hotel de la Paix.
__Tuesday__ Overcast & damp, but cleared up by lunch time & warmer now at tea time. We saw the abbey again [?It] was the bishop's mark. St. Philibert is [?it] buried here. We saw the upper room with 3 huge organ bellows, the crypt & the cloister. Lovely. And a beautiful "Brown Madonna", where I lit a candle for Olivia.
We wandered around the town - beautiful stone carved doorways & gutters (all clean) down the middle of cobbled streets.
We're now at St. Jean de Vaux. The sun is shining, although there might be thunder.
__Wednesday__
There was lots of thunder in the night. But th[e] full [?in] day h[as] had OK weather. Sometim[es]
pages.notes: Text continues from left page. 'Tournous' is underlined — likely a variant spelling or misread of 'Tournous'; could also be 'Tournus' (a town in Burgundy known for its Romanesque abbey of St. Philibert). The reference to St. Philibert being buried there strongly supports 'Tournus'. The '[?It]' and '[?it]' passages are partially legible small insertions above the line. The last line is cut off at the bottom of the scan. No handwritten page number visible at top-right corner.
